content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-4441          322-4444

January 12, 1984


Donald P. Gangemi
Norback, DuRard and Gangemi
181 Second Avenue, Suite 610
P.O. Box 2091
San Mateo, CA  94401

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-83-280

Dear Mr. Gangemi:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.  The facts, as
stated in your letter, are that you were recently elected to the
Board of Directors of the Half Moon Bay Fire Protection
District.  You are also a lawyer specializing in workers'
compensation who works as a salaried employee of a law firm.
You are a director of the firm.1/  The Half Moon Bay Fire
Protection District is currently negotiating salaries with the
firefighters' union.  In the last 24 months, you have
represented 14 members of the firefighters' union.2/  Your law
firm received 10 percent of each injured worker's recovery
benefits.  Recently, you were advised that your name is posted
at the Half Moon Bay firehouse as an attorney to be contacted in
the event of an industrial injury.  Your questions are:

     1.  Does the Political Reform Act allow you to participate
in the Board's salary negotiations with the firefighters' union?

     2.  Does the Act permit you to represent firefighters in the
Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District?3/

_______________
1/  You stated that your investment interest in the firm
is valued at less than $200.

2/  None of the 14 firefighters were employees of the Half
Moon Bay Fire Protection District.

3/  This question was raised during our telephone
conversation.

Donald P. Gangemi
January 12, 1984
Page 2


DISCUSSION

Government Code Section 871004/ prohibits a local public
official from making, participating in the making, or in any way
attempting to use his position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect5/ on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth more
than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

* * *

(c) Any source of income . . . aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to . . . the public official within 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director....

(Section 87103.)

The law firm which employs you is a source of income to you
and it is a business entity in which you are a director. You do
not have an investment interest in the firm because your
interest is worth less than $1,000, and the clients of the law
firm are not considered to be sources of income to you.6/

The Political Reform Act permits you to participate in the
salary negotiations with the firefighters' union. Based upon
the facts you have provided, it does not appear that the
decisions concerning the negotiations will have any effect upon
your law firm and, thus, the disqualification provisions will
not apply.

_________________________

4/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

5/ See the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702 for a discussion of the phrase "material financial
effect."

6/ See Section 82030.

Donald P. Gangemi
January 12, 1984
Page 3


The Act does not prohibit you from performing work for
individual Half Moon Bay firefighters. Assuming that they are
clients of your firm, and that any fees are paid to the firm and
not to you personally, the firefighters generally will not
become sources of income required to you, and you will not be required to
disqualify yourself on decisions affecting them.

An exception to the general rule is that you must disqualify
yourself if the Board is confronted with a decision affecting a
workman's compensation claim that is brought by a firefighter
which you represent through the firm. This disqualification is
required because 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(B)
provides that in the case of a source of income of $250 or more,
an official must disqualify himself from a decision if there is
a "nexus between the decision and the purpose for which the
official receives income." This regulation is intended to
prevent an official from accomplishing in his public capacity
what he is paid to do in his private capacity. Thus, as a Board
member, you may not make decisions affecting claims on which the
firm pays you to represent a client. (See the enclosed copy of
the Best advice letter, A-81-032.)

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:pln
Enclosure

LAW OFFICES

NORBACK, DURARD & GANGEMI
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
181 SECOND AVENUE, SUITE 810
POST OFFICE BOX 2091
SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA 94401
(415) 348-6741

ERNEST H. NORBACK
ROBERT J. DURARD
DONALD P. GANGEMI
DEBORAH E. ROSS
MICHAEL G. MCKENNA
J. KENNETH DONNELLY

JOHN G. CLARK
OF COUNSEL

December 16, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Ms. Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel

Dear Ms. Milman:

I was recently elected to the Board of Directors of the Half Moon
Bay Fire Protection District and was formally sworn in at the
December 1983 meeting. The Board is currently in salary
negotiations with its employees and the other two members of the
Board felt that I have a conflict of interest. Therefore,
pursuant to California Government Code Section 83114(b), it is
hereby requested that you render an opinion based on the
following facts:

1. My current employment.

I am employed as a salaried lawyer with Norback,
DuRard & Gangemi, a professional corporation. While I am a
Director of the Corporation, I own only a nominal interest in the
firm that I would estimate at less than $200.00.

I am a certified specialist in workers' compensation
and, to my knowledge, there are only three other attorney
certified specialists' (outside our firm) representing injured
workers in San Mateo County. Our office is located in the City
of San Mateo and we have no office within the jurisdiction of the
Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District.

2. My Clients.

Almost all my time is spent representing injured
workers before the Workers' Compensation Appeals Board and before
the U.S. Bureau of Hearings and Appeals (Social Security
Disability Benefits). My clients reside throughout the Bay Area.
Most are members of labor organizations although many are
referred to our firm from non-specialist attorneys, doctors,
former clients and through the telephone book yellow page

24

Ms. Barbara A. Milman
December 16, 1983
Page 2

directory.

Over the last 24 months I have agreed to represent
over 700 injured workers. Of that figure, less than 2% (or 12
firefighters) were members of the firefighters union and none
were employed by the Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District. To
my knowledge, I have never represented a Half Moon Bay
firefighter and feel that I could not do so while serving on the
Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District Board.

Under workers' compensation law, the attorney is
paid approximately 10% of the permanent disability benefits
awarded to the injured worker.

3. The Firefighters' Union.
The firefighters' union has members throughout San
Mateo County. They are represented in labor negotiations by a
law firm in San Francisco. Norback, DuRard & Gangemi have never
represented the union and have never received any monies
whatsoever from the union. The other members of the Half Moon
Bay Fire Protection District Board of Directors advised me that
my name had been posted in the Half Moon Bay firehouse by the
union as an attorney to be contacted in the event of an
industrial injury. I have made it clear that since assuming
office I could not represent any Half Moon Bay firefighters.

4. My Contention.
I am a salaried employee of a law firm in San Mateo
County with no financial connection with the firefighters' union.
Any decision I make as a member of the Board will not have a
material financial effect on a business entity such as Norback,
DuRard & Gangemi.

Your opinion is requested specifically on the issue of whether or
not I have a conflict of interest pursuant to Government Code
Section 87103 with respect to salary negotiations with the
firefighter union.

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b), I will not consider
or vote on any salary negotiations for 21 days. If you require

Ms. Barbara A. Milman
December 16, 1983
Page 3

additional information, please contact me.

Very truly yours,


DONALD P. GANGEMI
DPG/lf
OPE-3-AFL-CIO(199)
CC: Robert D. Tucker, President, Half Moon Bay
     Fire Protection District
     George Haupert, Secretary, Half Moon Bay
     Fire Protection District

sections.question: 1. Does the Political Reform Act allow you to participate in the Board's salary negotiations with the firefighters' union? 2. Does the Act permit you to represent firefighters in the Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District?
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act permits you to participate in the salary negotiations with the firefighters' union. The Act does not prohibit you from performing work for individual Half Moon Bay firefighters. An exception is that you must disqualify yourself if the Board is confronted with a decision affecting a workman's compensation claim that is brought by a firefighter which you represent through the firm.
sections.analysis:
Government Code Section 87100 4/ prohibits a local public 
official from maKing, participating in the making, or in any way 
attempting to use his position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
An official has a financial interest in a 
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect5/ on: 
(a) 
Any business entity in which tne public 
official has a direct or indirect investment worth more 
than one thousand dollars ($1,000).
sections.question_synthetic: Can an elected Fire Protection District Board member, who is a director at a law firm specializing in workers' compensation representation and has represented members of the firefighters' union in the past 24 months, participate in salary negotiations with the union and represent its members without violating conflict of interest provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that Mr. Gangemi may participate in salary negotiations with the firefighters' union because the decisions will not materially affect his law firm. He may also represent individual firefighters as clients, but must disqualify himself from Board decisions affecting workman's compensation claims he represents through the firm due to the nexus between the decision and his paid representation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Complete advice letter from January 12, 1984 with both incoming request (dated December 16, 1983) and FPPC response. Document includes clear Question and Conclusion sections with statutory citations and footnotes. Minor discrepancy noted: incoming letter states '12 firefighters' while FPPC response states '14 members' - FPPC appears to be referencing updated facts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does the Political Reform Act allow you to participate in the Board's salary negotiations with the firefighters' union? 2. Does the Act permit you to represent firefighters in the Half Moon Bay Fire Protection District?

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act permits you to participate in the salary negotiations with the firefighters' union. The Act does not prohibit you from performing work for individual Half Moon Bay firefighters. An exception is that you must disqualify yourself if the Board is confronted with a decision affecting a workman's compensation claim that is brought by a firefighter which you represent through the firm.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-83-280 addresses whether a newly elected Fire Protection District Board director and law firm director, who specializes in workers' compensation and has represented firefighters' union members, has a conflict of interest regarding salary negotiations and representation of firefighters. The FPPC concludes he may participate in salary negotiations and represent individual firefighters, but must recuse himself from decisions affecting workman's compensation claims he represents.
